mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling federal funds rate keeps the equity discount rate anchored, so S&P 500 prices reprice upward as investors extend the low-rate regime into the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down over prior 3 months, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above a recession-like threshold signals earnings downgrades and higher default risk, which should push S&P 500 lower over the next month as labor weakness propagates into consumer demand and margins.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 6.5% produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the median forward return is not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales indicates immediate demand destruction, which should translate into weaker near-term revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if one-week forward SPX returns are not negative on average after monthly RSAFS drops greater than 1.0%, or if the sign accuracy is below 55% over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and payrolls both improve together, the economy is expanding through real output and labor income at the same time, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and PAYEMS both weak in 2020-12 vintage
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform either series alone, or if positive 60-day returns are not observed in at least 55% of joint-expansion cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above a recovery threshold indicate stronger construction demand and a wealth-effect impulse to consumption, which should lift equity prices over the following month through improved growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above the 6-month moving average are followed by non-positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the forward-return mean is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in CPI while the policy rate remains pinned near zero raises real-rate repression and the risk of future tightening, which should pressure equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A positive gap between industrial production and payroll growth signals productivity-led expansion rather than labor hoarding, which should support margin outlooks and positive S&P 500 returns over two to three months.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows faster than PAYEMS over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 105.7244 and PAYEMS vintage 142624.0 on 2020-12
failure_condition: Reject if periods where INDPRO outpaces PAYEMS do not lead to above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the spread loses significance after controlling for overall growth level.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDP, retail sales, and housing starts identifies a genuine demand rebound across consumption, income, and household formation, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage/revision gap is large at 2020-07
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements across GDPC1, RSAFS, and HOUST fail to outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of synchronized rebound instances.
